name: write_commutative_different_addrs
code:
proof fn phi_1_write_commutative_different_addrs(mem: PTMem, a1: usize, v1: usize, a2: usize, v2: usize)
    requires
        a1 != a2,
    ensures
        mem.write(a1, v1).write(a2, v2) == mem.write(a2, v2).write(a1, v1),
{
}
reason: Writes to different addresses should commute — if they don't, the write_seq fold_left order matters even for disjoint addresses, which would be a spec gap

name: write_overwrites_same_addr
reason: A second write to the same address should fully overwrite the first — tests that Map::insert idempotency holds for PTMem, which is foundational for store buffer reasoning

    ensures
        L3_ENTRY_SIZE == PAGE_SIZE,
        L2_ENTRY_SIZE == 512 * PAGE_SIZE,
